title: Monday Night May 11th 1846 / Domestic Peace by Anne Bronte — Sad Poetry Lines
content:
Why should such gloomy silence reign;
And why is all the house so drear,
When neither danger, sickness, pain,
Nor death, nor want have entered here?
We are as many as we were
That other night, when all were gay,
And full of hope, and free from care;
Yet, is there something gone away.
meaning: "Monday Night May 11th 1846 / Domestic Peace" by Anne Bronte is a sad and love and deep and inspirational poem consisting of 33 lines. The poet explores themes of sad and love and deep and inspirational through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "Why should such gloomy silence reign; And why is all the house so drear,...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Anne Bronte's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
